# Ygrove Winter-Hand

## Material Reality

Ygrove eats the cold-north clan baseline (EVERYDAY_LIFE.md §(c)) — oats, rye, dried fish, mutton, hard cheese, dried berries, mead — in Cluster C proportion: her daily bulk-plant requirement is substantial per PEOPLES Cluster C; hearth-stewed root-vegetable mash is ordinary. She drinks mead ritually and small spirits on winter-fever nights. Her clan-hall adjacent residence is a sized-for-Cluster-C longhouse with reinforced floors and oversized doorways (PEOPLES Cluster C architecture). Her sleeping chamber has heavy peat-stove heating.

She has known every woolly-rhinoceros-folk who has passed through her clan-hall for forty years. Her body shows the characteristic Cluster C heat-vulnerability in summer-visit to heartland but winter-cold suits her.

Possessions: sanction-staff of carved birch; clan-ring of her mother's mother; the burnished axe she carried as a young warrior before her hands grew too slow; winter-woven over-robe; her husband's hearth-ring.

She cannot travel to heartland for practical reasons (Cluster C heat-stress + clan-authority weight). She cannot easily visit her son in the adopting polity without announcing a visit that the clan-saga-register cannot accommodate. She cannot revoke her son's pending sanction without the clan-council's concurrence; she cannot confirm it without naming him in the saga-register that her own precedent from twenty winters ago would contradict if known.

Her mobility is clan-ground and adjacent clan-territories by walking or clan-horse; she has not crossed the southern pass in 30 winters and would not in her remaining time.

She owes nothing standing. Clan-custom in-kind economy; kin-primacy binds her more than any contract.

Her woolly-rhinoceros-folk embodiment: Cluster C size and strength (PEOPLES — large body plan, architecturally significant); characteristic heat-vulnerability in summer; winter-adapted coat; slow-and-powerful movement registering authority in clan-hall standing; voice carries at low-register distance. Her 80-120 Cluster C lifespan places her at late-middle age; she has begun the slowness.

## Institutional Embedding

**Family / clan / household:** widowed 20 winters; son emigrated 3 winters; daughter clan-hall adjacent; deceased daughter 12 winters; nephew on CF-0032 subsection reciprocal-journey circuit; extended clan-kin across three adjacent clan territories.

**Law / custom / judgment:** clan-custom sovereign in clan-ground (INSTITUTIONS §Law; highland clan-ground thin chartered-court reach per GEOGRAPHY); her clan-hall is the sanctioning venue; she presides. No civic-registry standing outside clan-territory.

**Religion / ritual:** progenitor-cult lineage-keeper for woolly-rhinoceros-folk lineage in her clan (PEOPLES Cluster C + INSTITUTIONS §Religion); common-pantheon minimum-observance for cross-clan visitors; CF-0032 CH-0006 firewall respected (progenitor-cult lineage-keeper integration of oversize-saddlery subsection prestige is sectarian activity, not clan-chartered endorsement); CF-0036 CH-0010 firewall respected (does not assert supremacist doctrine).

**Employer / guild:** none; CF-0032 subsection-circuit nephew is in the guild-recognized circuit but her authority is clan-custom not guild.

**Military / defense:** clan levy retired; her clan's border-watch is her daughter-adjacent cousin's responsibility now.

**Debt:** none; in-kind economy.

**Local taboo:** CF-0036 supremacist-doctrine recruiter-pamphlets she has ritually burned in clan-hall twice; highland exclusionary-settlement distinction respected (her clan is NOT exclusionary — CF-0036 single-species enclave distinct from clan-custom community); common-pantheon minimum not mocked; cross-cluster courtesy-minimum in clan-hall when heartland visitors come.

**Literacy:** oral-primary; trade-tongue literate for inter-clan correspondence; DIS-2 partial literacy at upper-clan-elder stratum; clan-saga oral-tradition fully literate.

**Inheritance:** clan-authority would pass to her daughter if the daughter stays; would pass to her sister's eldest-daughter otherwise; her personal goods (sanction-staff, clan-ring, axe, over-robe) per clan-custom to the inheritor.

**Landholding:** clan-custom common; clan-territory held in common by the clan under her sanction-authority.

**Healing:** clan-herb-wife reachable; her own body she triages herself; no barber-surgeon within clan-ground.

**Burial:** progenitor-cult sky-adjacent rite in the high-ridge burial circle.

**Recordkeeping:** clan-saga oral-tradition (she is primary carrier); clan-ledger wax-tablet for inter-clan correspondence; no civic-registry entry.

## Epistemic Position

**Known firsthand:** 50 years of clan-sanction-ritual; 40 years of clan-council observation; her husband's private-sanction-revocation 20 winters; her mother's clan-saga-fragment lineage (received at her mother's death 30 winters ago); two CF-0036 supremacist-recruiter pamphlet burnings in her own clan-hall; named tales of outland-band cousins across two generations (CF-0034 in highland register); clan demography across three adjacent territories; her son's childhood and emigration letter; her daughter's wavering posture over the past three winters.

**Known by rumor:** CF-0033 dragoon-adoption politics in adopting polities (she knows the son-emigrated-to-dragoon pattern in three other clan-territories); CF-0032 subsection reciprocal-circuit politics (through her nephew); CF-0043 Ash-Seal defector-rumor (barely; through an autumn-season heartland visitor); heartland Charter-Era civic-registry patterns she has heard discussed without engaging.

**Cannot know:** M-1 / M-2 / M-5 / M-9 Maker-mystery territory beyond clan-folk-layer; CAU-3 ward-specifics; CF-0021 / CF-0023 crafter-material (no highland exposure in her direct register); CF-0042 status-display-economy (heartland-elite register foreign to her); specific Brinewick civic politics.

**Wrongly believes:** M-1 "consumed themselves" folk reading at rumor-layer (PERMITTED common-interpretation); M-4 species-progenitor figures as her-species-lineage-adjacent (PERMITTED common-interpretation; she does NOT assert historical-mythical-aspectual resolution per M-4 firewall); CF-0032 antiquity registered through M-12 permitted-reading.

**Vocabulary:** trade-tongue + highland-vernacular + clan-saga register + sanction-ritual speech + progenitor-cult lineage-keeping vocabulary + inter-clan correspondence formal-register.

**Vocabulary she lacks:** ward-inscription guild-internal (CAU-3); crafter vessel-preparation (CF-0021); Maker-linguistic (CF-0023); heartland civic-court procedural; Brinewick canal-quarter addressing; fenland marker-reed; drylands verse-register; estate-dowager interior.

## Goals and Pressures

**Short-term goal:** decide her son's sanction before the next clan-council (one moon away).

**Long-term desire:** pass clan-authority to her daughter intact with tradition-integrity publicly preserved.

**Unavoidable obligation:** clan-custom absolute; kin-primacy; CF-0026 highland-variant lineage-keeper carrier role.

**External pressure:** son's emigration + daughter's wavering + CF-0033 southern pressure + CF-0036 recruiter-material arrival + CF-0032 nephew-circuit reports from heartland + clan demographic decline.

**Public mask:** stone-solid immutable clan-matriarch; declarative sanction-register.

**Private appetite:** one selective leniency that preserves tradition by exception (precisely the register her private breach 20 winters ago already used).

**Social fear:** the saga that names her next for revoking the sanction.

**Private shame:** the revocation 20 winters ago for her husband. She chose to save him from the saga-register at cost of the tradition's integrity; he died anyway; she has not been able to revoke the private revocation because the precedent it set is load-bearing for her interior.

**Central contradiction:** she is the tradition's keeper AND has privately broken it. Every public sanction reaffirms an integrity she has not held in twenty winters. Her son's pending sanction is the external form: confirm and lose the son; revoke openly and reveal the private breach.

**Repeated forced choice:** kinship-vs-law (clan-custom functions as law at her level); loyalty-vs-evidence (tradition-integrity vs what she knows she did).

## Capabilities

### Clan-sanction ritual-authority

- **how_learned:** kin-lineage progression received at her mother's death 30 winters ago; 50-year accrued observation-and-practice including her pre-matriarch period
- **cost_to_acquire:** the authority is the cost (she cannot step aside without revoking the saga-register); 20 winters of carrying the private breach
- **teachers_institutions:** her mother (her predecessor); clan-council observation; inherited clan-saga oral-tradition
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for clan-elder rank per PEOPLES Cluster C + INSTITUTIONS §Family + CF-0026 highland-variant
- **body_class_place_shape:** Cluster C species-embodiment commanding clan-hall presence; clan-territory place-shape; stone-authority body-register

### Clan-council parliamentary fluency

- **how_learned:** 40 years observation + 30 years practice
- **cost_to_acquire:** long tenure
- **teachers_institutions:** clan-council itself
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for mid-senior clan-elders
- **body_class_place_shape:** species-embodiment authority + clan-hall geography

### Clan-saga oral-tradition keeping

- **how_learned:** lineage-transmission from her mother; accumulated through 50 years of ritual performance
- **cost_to_acquire:** deep memory-work; ongoing composition under clan-council sanction
- **teachers_institutions:** her mother; clan-saga tradition
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for the matriarch carrier role
- **body_class_place_shape:** voice-register carries at low-frequency distance (species-embodiment); clan-hall acoustic geometry

### Inter-clan correspondence literacy (trade-tongue)

- **how_learned:** clan-custom training in her young-adult period; 40 years of practice
- **cost_to_acquire:** deliberate acquisition outside oral-primary clan custom
- **teachers_institutions:** a trade-tongue tutor her mother engaged 50 winters back
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for her rank; partial — oral-primary remains her stronger register
- **body_class_place_shape:** species-neutral skill

### Inter-species diplomatic register (highland commerce threshold)

- **how_learned:** accumulated through cross-cluster highland commerce contacts across 40 years
- **cost_to_acquire:** time and measured register-practice
- **teachers_institutions:** cross-cluster visitors; clan-border trade-custom
- **unusual_or_ordinary:** ORDINARY for border-clan elders
- **body_class_place_shape:** Cluster C species-presence calibrated against Cluster A/B visitor-scale

## Voice and Perception

**Preferred metaphors:** stone-register (stone-solid, stone-footed, the stone beneath the snow); peat-fire register (the hearth, the long burn, the coal beneath ash); frost-line register (the line the winter draws, the line the saga draws); winter-hand register (the cold grip, the slow clench); clan-saga declarative-formulaic in public; kin-interior shorter and softer.

**Rhythm of speech:** slow-cadence, declarative-stoned; silences that carry weight she does not need to fill; clan-saga formulaic when ritual; softer-contracted when alone with her daughter.

**Taboo words:** her husband's private-sanction-revocation (never); her son's name in recent tense; supremacist-doctrine recruiter register (absolute refusal); any heroic inflation of her own past (AES-1 CH-0016 firewall respected); the specific phrasing of the 20-winter-ago revocation (she has said it aloud once and does not again).

**What she notices first:** arrival-cadence of a clan-hall visitor (she knows every clan-pair's walk); the particular silence after a name no one expects to hear (her son's, for the past three winters); which younger clan-member is studying the recruiter-pamphlet too carefully; the hearth-fire's register when the clan-council is about to turn.

**What she overlooks:** her own body's slow decline (she does not name it); her daughter's private grief at the clan-sanction pending on her brother (the daughter will not speak of it and Ygrove has not asked).

**Species perception effects:** Cluster C low-frequency hearing (her clan-hall-carrying voice returns to her at distance she reads for arrival-cadence); heat-vulnerability makes her noticeable in any summer-crossing; large-body-plan visibility in clan-territory geography; slow-powerful movement that people read as authority.

## Contradictions and Tensions

Her authority's integrity requires her secret to stay; her son's situation demands the revocation her secret is the precedent for; the tradition she keeps is not one she has fully held in twenty winters. Her daughter, if she stays, inherits the breach she does not know about; if she leaves, inherits nothing but the clan's quiet.

## Likely Story Hooks

- **The clan-council requires her sanction ruling on her son.** She has composed two versions: one confirms; one revokes publicly (which exposes the twenty-winter precedent). She has not chosen.
- **Her daughter announces emigration intent.** The clan-authority succession fails; the inheritor-cousin would receive without the clan-saga-keeping Ygrove has trained her daughter for.
- **A recruiter-pamphlet is found in her nephew's saddlebag** on his return from the CF-0032 subsection circuit. She must burn it publicly AND investigate privately — her nephew is young; the circuit-peer-pressure is real.
- **Her son writes and she does not open the letter.** The letter sits in her sanction-staff cavity for a winter. Her daughter finds it and asks.
- **The clan's winter-fever season takes someone whose passing requires her sanction-speech for burial.** The speech requires her to name the deceased in the clan-saga-register. The deceased is adjacent-kin to her emigrated son.
- **A CF-0032 journeyman-circuit visitor brings news of the heartland subsection's dowager-gossip circuit** — linking to NCP-0004 mammoth-folk dowager in Brinewick. A cross-polity visit invitation follows. Ygrove's Cluster C heat-vulnerability and her clan-authority weight make the visit prohibitive; the invitation itself is the social-test.
